content.full_text:
February 15, 2007

James H. Jennings
33346 Chamizo Calle
Aguadulce, CA 91390

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-07-010

Dear Mr. Jennings:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the applicability of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 Regulation 18329.5(a)(3)(A) (copy enclosed) provides that the Fair Political Practices Commission (the “Commission”) will render formal written advice or informal assistance regarding the interpretation of an agency’s conflict of interest code or the application of that code to a specific individual only if he or she first requests a determination from the agency and the code reviewing body for such conflict of interest code.2 This provision is important because the code reviewing body for a particular state or local agency adopts that body’s duties under the Act. (Sections 87300-87312.) The code reviewing body would also determine what duties actors within a particular agency would have. Unless you comply with this regulation, the Commission is not permitted to render advice. Because, according to your letter, you did not comply with this requirement, your request has been withdrawn.

As we discussed on the phone, you will first need to contact the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors to get their determination on this issue. When you make an inquiry to that body, it might be helpful if you attach this letter for their information.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 The Commission is not the code reviewing body for your town council. We must refer you to the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors. (Regulation 18329.5(a)(3)(B), copy enclosed.)
